input: User connects with valid JWT token
explanation: Successful connection returns confirmation with user and socket IDs.
User joins the room and receives subscription confirmation.

input: send('user1', { type: 'order-shipped', title: 'Order Shipped', body: 'Your order has shipped' })
output: User1 receives: { event: 'notification', data: { id: '...', type: 'order-shipped', ... } }
explanation: Targeted notification is delivered to the specific user's active WebSocket connections.

input: broadcast('order-updates', { type: 'sale', title: 'Flash Sale' }) with 3 users subscribed
output: All 3 subscribed users receive the notification
explanation: Room broadcast delivers the notification to all room members.

input: send('offlineUser', { type: 'message', title: 'New Message' }); offlineUser connects later
output: On connection, offlineUser receives the buffered notification
explanation: Notifications for offline users are buffered and delivered when they reconnect.

input: User connects from two browser tabs simultaneously
output: Both sockets receive notifications sent to that user
explanation: Multiple connections per user are supported; notifications go to all active sockets.

input: User disconnects, other users are notified
output: Online users receive: { event: 'presence', userId: 'user1', status: 'offline' }
explanation: Presence tracking broadcasts online/offline status changes to other connected users.
Acknowledgement confirms delivery and removes the notification from retry queue.

input: Send same notification ID twice to same user
explanation: Deduplication prevents the same notification from being delivered multiple times.

output: Oldest notification dropped, 100 most recent notifications kept
explanation: Offline buffer enforces a per-user limit, dropping oldest notifications when exceeded.
